message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make compose render ALL 346 baskets fast and faithful. The ceiling is SERIALIZATION, NOT the OpenRouter rate limit (proven: 0 429s at 128-way concurrency). The 0615bc5 commit only fixed a secondary path (abstractive_writer.py); the AUTHORITATIVE compose path still runs sync-on-event-loop at achieved concurrency 1.0.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: Achieved compose concurrency >1 (measured via an event-loop heartbeat probe: max loop-gap during a section's phase-2 must be <2s where it previously froze for minutes) AND two sections' phase-2 progress interleaving AND the full-corpus render time trending down — WITHOUT any change to NLI/strict_verify faithfulness semantics (kept/dropped verdict set must stay identical to a serial control).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Verified worktree state: /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix @ HEAD 5d27115 (branch bot/compose-fix), git clean. The mission's '4 uncommitted files' were already committed in prior rounds (1f9da4c off-loop 4 hot spots, 7d23a11 record/replay A/B, 5d27115 full-corpus launcher). The section_polish.py DEAD-constants concern was already resolved in round 1 (constants dropped, per WHEEL_PROGRESS).",
    "RECONCILED 329 vs 346: '346' is a PHANTOM — it appears in NO authoritative checkpoint as a basket count. The only '346' hits in cp3 are an incidental evidence id (ev_346) and a hash (cg_c08b73460437). Authoritative cp3 = 329 baskets (payload.baskets len 329; consolidation_summary.basket_total=329).",
    "PINNED the 329→328 reconstruction delta to EXACTLY 1 basket (ev_621): a singleton whose sole member evidence id is ABSENT from cp2's evidence_for_gen pool (screened upstream at s2), so there is no source text to quote/verify. Legitimate pre-compose screen (build_baskets 'if not members: continue', run_s5_i3.py:64), NOT a compose loss. All 328 composable baskets are mapped, stranded=0: {0:15,1:12,2:14,3:13,4:274}=328.",
    "Confirmed ZERO real HTTP 429s: all 8 '429' log hits are incidental (timestamps like 20:31:15,429; '429 reasoning tokens'; '429 chunks'). Zero Tracebacks, zero abandoned baskets.",
    "Verified mechanism criteria in live fullcorpus.log: phase2_max_concurrent=4 (PHASE2 ENTER concurrent 1→2→3→4, all off_loop_thread=True), FOUR sections' phase-2 interleaving; hb_max_loop_gap=2.7s in a SINGLE freeze 82s BEFORE phase-2 (zero phase-2 freezes) vs pre-fix minutes-long phase-2 freeze.",
    "Confirmed sections 0/1/2/3 all DONE (walls: sec1=758s, sec3=1086s, sec2=1578s, sec0=1643s; phase-2 dts 616.2/972.9/1402.4/1475.1s). Section 4 (274-basket long pole) entered phase-2 at ~20:39 and is STILL running at 20:50 (remaining_concurrent=1).",
    "Established the section-4-only rerun mechanism for the LLM-concurrency lever: probe forwards sys.argv to run_s5_i3.main, which accepts --sections 4 (and --cap-primary N for a bounded disclosed subset), enabling a clean isolated A/B."
  ],
  "commits": [],
  "files": [],
  "exercised": "Read live outputs/s5_fullcorpus/fullcorpus.log from the RUNNING full-corpus render (pid 1106221, launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh @ PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=4, HB_MODE=offloop) — not py_compile. Reconciliation was exercised by re-deriving build_baskets over the real cp2/cp3 JSON in python3 (329 baskets in, 328 out, exactly 1 dropped = ev_621, confirmed absent from cp2 evidence_for_gen). Mechanism metrics read directly from probe-emitted PHASE2 ENTER/EXIT + HB FREEZE lines in the live log.\", \n",
  "measurement": "FULL-CORPUS WALL NOT YET CAPTURED — render is live at report time (forced structured-output). Section 4 (274 baskets, the dominant long pole) is still in phase-2 after ~11 min while all 4 other sections completed. What IS measured from live fullcorpus.log artifacts: phase2_max_concurrent=4 (>1, criterion MET), hb_max_loop_gap=2.7s single pre-phase-2 freeze with ZERO phase-2 freezes (<2s criterion MET), 4 sections' phase-2 interleaving (MET), 0 real HTTP 429s. Per-section phase-2 walls at PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16: sec1=616.2s, sec3=972.9s, sec0/sec2=1402.4s/1475.1s. Reconciliation: authoritative corpus=329 baskets (NOT 346, a phantom), 328 composable + 1 legitimate pre-compose screen (ev_621, source screened at s2). Full wall + the 16→48 lever A/B are pending baseline completion; two background watchers (buqmu29or, bte5wei8j) are armed to notify on PROBE SUMMARY or process death.",
  "open": [
    "Full-corpus PROBE SUMMARY wall not yet captured — section 4 (274-basket long pole) still in phase-2; watchers buqmu29or + bte5wei8j armed to capture wall_s/phase2_max_concurrent/hb_max_loop_gap_s on completion.",
    "Assembled-report sanity check (all 5 sections present, kept/dropped counts plausible) pending render completion + cp5_generation_snapshot.json write.",
    "PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM 16→48 edit at launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh:22 NOT yet applied (deferred to avoid touching the file mid-render; edit does not affect the already-loaded process but kept clean).",
    "Section-4 lever A/B (16 vs 48) NOT yet run — blocked on baseline wall.",
    "WHEEL_PROGRESS.md not yet updated with wall + reconciliation (needs the real wall number first)."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS",
  "blockers": [
    "Hard time/latency blocker: the section-4 long pole (274 baskets) phase-2 is genuinely slow at PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16 and had not finished when the harness forced StructuredOutput. The top-line wall is a function of this render completing."
  ]
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
For the PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM 16→48 'trending down' A/B: section 4 shared the GLOBAL 16-slot LLM semaphore with sections 0/2 during its phase-2 in this full run, so section-4's in-run dt is contention-confounded. Is the clean lever measurement an ISOLATED section-4-only run at 16 vs 48 (--sections 4, removing the cross-section semaphore confound) rather than section-4-in-full-run vs full-run-at-48 — and is a --cap-primary N bounded subset (disclosed non-acceptance, ~1/4 compute) acceptable for that lever benchmark, given faithfulness/verdict-set identity was already certified separately by the record/replay A/B and does not depend on basket count?

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "\"Real 2-section A/B on the authoritative compose path (heartbeat probe, cap-primary 2). CONTROL (sync-on-loop, pre-fix): phase2_max_concurrent=1, max event-loop gap=301.47s, sections serial, wall=486.7s. TREATMENT (off-loop fix): phase2_max_concurrent=2 (two off-loop worker threads, phase-2 overlapping), max loop-gap=0.55s (<2s bar MET), wall=269.6s. Loop-gap progression as the 4 offloads landed: 15.24s -> 4.60s -> 10.01s -> 0.55s. Env knobs proven read: resolve_max_concurrency()==16, _parallel_verify_workers()==8.\"",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Add a record/replay shim to /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/scripts/_hb_probe_run.py: one recording pass captures every verify_sentence_provenance/strict_verify judge request→response keyed by content hash plus canned writer_fn drafts; then replay the SAME drafts+judge answers through both arms — serial control (to_thread inlined) and treatment at PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 — and assert byte-identical kept/dropped sentence sets (this certifies the 4 offloads under real thread concurrency at zero LLM cost). On pass, set PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=4 and launch the full 346-basket render via /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh with the heartbeat probe attached to take the top-line corpus number."
  },
  {
    "r": 2,
    "meas": "RECORD/REPLAY A/B (zero LLM on replay, real code, real thread concurrency): record judge_keys=20 writer_keys=7 divergent_judge_keys=0; both replay arms 23 judge + 8 writer calls with 0 replay-miss; section 0 verified=1 dropped=1 (a real KEEP and a real DROP) -> IDENTICAL sha both arms; assembled_report_md byte-identical (sha df569cee, 812B). VERDICT-SET IDENTITY: PASS (rc=0). FULL-CORPUS CONCURRENCY (launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh, PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=4, 329 baskets/5 sections): achieved compose concurrency = 4 (PHASE2 ENTER 1->2->3->4, off_loop_thread=True), up from pre-fix 1.0; max phase-2 event-loop gap <2.0s (ZERO HB FREEZE during phase-2; the only >2s gap was a single 2.7s startup blip 82s BEFORE the first phase-2 entry) vs the pre-fix 301s phase-2 freeze; four sections' phase-2 interleaving. ABSOLUTE full-corpus wall NOT yet available — render still running in background (residual section 4 = 274 baskets is the long pole and is now gated by PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16, i.e. the ceiling moved from the event loop to LLM concurrency); writer wall_cap=1700s guarantees termination.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Do not touch the running render. When it completes (monitor bwst15ovd is armed): capture the PROBE SUMMARY wall from outputs/s5_fullcorpus/fullcorpus.log, sanity-check the assembled report (all 5 sections present, kept/dropped counts plausible), reconcile 329 rendered baskets vs the mission's 346 (find where the 17 are filtered and confirm it is a legitimate pre-compose screen, not a loss), and commit the wall number + reconciliation to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. Then raise PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM from 16 to 48 at launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh:22 (0 429s proven at 128-way; leave headroom below 128) and take a timed rerun of the dominant residual-section-4 workload to move the new LLM-concurrency ceiling — that rerun's wall vs this run's wall is the clean 'trending down' A/B. No pre-fix full-render control is required; the round-1 serial-vs-off-loop wall (486.7s→269.6s) already certifies direction."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? (Achieved compose concurrency >1 (measured via an event-loop heartbeat probe: max loop-gap during a section's phase-2 must be <2s where it previously froze for minutes) AND two sections' phase-2 progress interleaving AND the full-corpus render time trending down — WITHOUT any change to NLI/strict_verify faithfulness semantics (kept/dropped verdict set must stay identical to a serial control).)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command: tail -n 40 /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/outputs/s5_fullcorpus/fullcorpus.log 2>/dev/null; echo ---; ps -p 1106221 -o pid,etime,cmd 2>/dev/null || echo "pid 1106221 gone"
input.description: Check full-corpus render log tail and process status

input.command: cd /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix && git log --oneline -5 && git status --short | head -20

input.gap_to_top: Three of four criteria are MET with real artifacts (phase2_max_concurrent=4 >1; zero phase-2 loop-gap freezes vs pre-fix 301s; four sections interleaving; verdict-set identity replay-certified). The remaining gap is the top-line number itself: the full-corpus PROBE SUMMARY wall has never been captured (section 4, the 274-basket long pole, is STILL in phase-2 at pid 1106221, elapsed 33:40 as of this check), and the 'full-corpus render time trending down' criterion therefore has a direction-only proof (round-1 capped A/B 486.7s→269.6s) but no full-corpus baseline or post-lever wall. Also 346 is now established as a phantom — authoritative corpus is 329 baskets, 328 composable + 1 legitimate s2 screen (ev_621) — so the mission's '346' should be recorded as reconciled to 329 in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.
